verses.text: Balaam builds seven altars and offers, then seeks the LORD. Altars. He goes through the forms.
Balaam builds seven altars and offers, then seeks the LORD. Altars. He goes through the forms.
Balaam builds seven altars and offers, then seeks the LORD. Altars. He goes through the forms.
God puts a word in his mouth. The word. He must speak God's message.
God puts a word in his mouth. The word. He must speak God's message.
God puts a word in his mouth. The word. He must speak God's message.
He blesses: 'How shall I curse whom God hath not cursed?' First oracle. He cannot reverse God's blessing.
He blesses: 'How shall I curse whom God hath not cursed?' First oracle. He cannot reverse God's blessing.
He blesses: 'How shall I curse whom God hath not cursed?' First oracle. He cannot reverse God's blessing.
He blesses: 'How shall I curse whom God hath not cursed?' First oracle. He cannot reverse God's blessing.
Balak protests; Balaam says he must speak God's word. Bound. He cannot do otherwise.
Balak protests; Balaam says he must speak God's word. Bound. He cannot do otherwise.
Balak takes him to another place to try again. Another try. He hopes a new view will help.
Balak takes him to another place to try again. Another try. He hopes a new view will help.
Balak takes him to another place to try again. Another try. He hopes a new view will help.
God meets Balaam and gives him a word. Met. God again rules his mouth.
God meets Balaam and gives him a word. Met. God again rules his mouth.
'God is not a man, that he should lie... hath he said, and shall he not do it?' Second oracle. God's word and blessing are sure.
'God is not a man, that he should lie... hath he said, and shall he not do it?' Second oracle. God's word and blessing are sure.
'God is not a man, that he should lie... hath he said, and shall he not do it?' Second oracle. God's word and blessing are sure.
He sees no iniquity in Jacob; God is with them as a king. Blessed. God will not curse His own.
He sees no iniquity in Jacob; God is with them as a king. Blessed. God will not curse His own.
He sees no iniquity in Jacob; God is with them as a king. Blessed. God will not curse His own.
He sees no iniquity in Jacob; God is with them as a king. Blessed. God will not curse His own.
Balak is angry; Balaam repeats he must obey God. Firm. He will not bend.
Balak is angry; Balaam repeats he must obey God. Firm. He will not bend.
Balak takes him to Peor for a third attempt. Once more. He will not give up cursing.
Balak takes him to Peor for a third attempt. Once more. He will not give up cursing.
Balak takes him to Peor for a third attempt. Once more. He will not give up cursing.
Balak takes him to Peor for a third attempt. Once more. He will not give up cursing.
